group.label: Rate Limiting
group.comment: Rate limiting can be applied globally to all Magewire requests, and/or individually per component. Global settings take precedence over component-specific limits when both are configured.

field.label: Enable in Developer Mode
field.comment: Rate limiting is always enforced in production mode. In developer or default mode it is skipped unless this is set to "Yes". Leave on "No" to avoid throttling during local development and automated tests.

field.label: Variant
field.comment:
Requests only – limits the number of update requests within the configured time window (see the "Requests" settings below), either shared across all components or isolated per component depending on the chosen scope.
Components only – limits requests per component using each component's own configured limit, ignoring the global "Requests" settings.
Requests and Components are mutually exclusive; pick the one that matches whether you want a single global budget or per-component budgets.

group.label: Requests
group.comment: Note: These settings only take effect when "requests" rate limiting is enabled.

field.label: Scope
field.comment:
Shared – one budget for the whole page; update requests from every component count against the same Max Attempts limit.
Isolated – each component gets its own Max Attempts budget, so a busy component cannot exhaust the limit for the others.

field.label: Max Attempts
field.comment: Maximum number of requests allowed globally within the decay seconds period configured below.

field.label: Decay Seconds
field.comment: Time window in seconds for the max attempts limit above. After this period, the attempt counter resets.
